Notice: Function _load_textdomain_just_in_time was called incorrectly. Translation loading for the astra-addon domain was triggered too early. This is usually an indicator for some code in the plugin or theme running too early. Translations should be loaded at the init action or later. Please see Debugging in WordPress for more information. (This message was added in version 6.7.0.) in C:\Users\GM Team\Local Sites\location-wise-product\app\public\wp-includes\functions.php on line 6131

Settings

Multi Location Product & Inventory Management for WooCommerce Pro

Manage products, inventory, and pricing across multiple store locations effortlessly

Pro Version

Quick Start Guide:

1

Create Locations

Set up your store locations with names, addresses, and contact details

2

Assign Products

Link products to locations and manage stock, pricing, and availability

3

Enable Location Selector

Let customers choose their preferred location and see relevant products

View Demo View Documentation Get Support
Unlimited Locations
Regular Updates
Premium Support

Settings

GeneralInventoryProduct VisibilityPopupOrder & CartNotificationsLocation Wise EverythingUser ExperienceLocation Info ManagementText ManagementAdvancedPlugin License

Display Location in Product Title Settings

Configure how store locations appear with product titles.

Location Display Format
Title-Location Separator

The character(s) used to separate the title and location.

Show Location On


Branding & Styling Settings

Customize the appearance and styling of popups, buttons, and other frontend elements to match your brand identity.

Popup Background Color

Background color for popup/modal panels.

Popup Text Color

Text color for popup/modal content.

Popup Overlay Background

Background color for popup overlay (supports rgba format).

Primary Color

Primary color used for buttons, gradients, and main interactive elements. This replaces the button background color.

Secondary Color

Secondary color used for button hover states, gradients, and complementary elements. This replaces the button hover background color.

Border Color

Border color for popups, cards, inputs, and other bordered elements.

Button Text Color

Text color for buttons.

Base Font Size

Base font size (e.g., 15px, 16px, 1rem).

Font Family

Font family for popup and frontend elements.

Box Shadow

Box shadow for popups and cards (e.g., 0 18px 45px rgba(24, 24, 24, 0.18)).

Border Radius

Border radius for popups and buttons (e.g., 20px, 10px, 5px).

Branding Preview

Your branding settings will be applied to all popups, buttons, and frontend elements. Changes take effect immediately after saving.

Sample Button:

Popup Settings

Configure Popup settings for location-based stock and price management.

Enable Popup

Enable or disable popup management.

Template Selection

Select the template for location selector.

Use Select2

Use select2 instead of normal select

Title Show in Popup

Show title in popup modal

Hierarchical Option
Show Count

Show count in popup

Show Popup for Admins

Show popup for admin users

Popup Custom Css
Text Management

Text Management Overview

Update every customer-facing label, prompt, and message from one place. Each section groups related text so it is clear where the copy appears on the storefront.

Enable Text Management

When disabled, custom text overrides are ignored and plugin defaults are used.

Quick Translate

Apply a preset language pack to all text fields.

Applying a language will replace current text values. Save changes to keep them.

Reset Text

Restore the original defaults for all text fields.

Reset only the Text Management fields to their defaults.

Modal & Popup

Text shown in location selector popups and modal layouts.

Main heading shown in all popup layouts. Disabled because Text Management is turned off. Frontend text will use defaults.

Subtitle shown across all popup layouts. Disabled because Text Management is turned off. Frontend text will use defaults.

Placeholder for the default popup dropdown. Disabled because Text Management is turned off. Frontend text will use defaults.

Placeholder for deeper hierarchical dropdowns. Disabled because Text Management is turned off. Frontend text will use defaults.

Primary action button text across popup layouts and selection lists. Disabled because Text Management is turned off. Frontend text will use defaults.

Label above the search input in modern layouts. Disabled because Text Management is turned off. Frontend text will use defaults.

Placeholder inside the popup search input across layouts. Disabled because Text Management is turned off. Frontend text will use defaults.

Button label for starting a popup search. Disabled because Text Management is turned off. Frontend text will use defaults.

Button label for geolocation lookup. Disabled because Text Management is turned off. Frontend text will use defaults.

Heading above the nearest store section. Disabled because Text Management is turned off. Frontend text will use defaults.

Heading above the additional locations list. Disabled because Text Management is turned off. Frontend text will use defaults.

Label above the classic popup search input. Disabled because Text Management is turned off. Frontend text will use defaults.

Shown while the popup is trying to detect the customer location. Disabled because Text Management is turned off. Frontend text will use defaults.

Shown while searching for locations. Disabled because Text Management is turned off. Frontend text will use defaults.

Shown when a popup search request fails. Disabled because Text Management is turned off. Frontend text will use defaults.

Shown when geolocation fails in popup layouts. Disabled because Text Management is turned off. Frontend text will use defaults.

Shown when there are no locations to display. Disabled because Text Management is turned off. Frontend text will use defaults.

Shown when a popup search returns no results. Disabled because Text Management is turned off. Frontend text will use defaults.

Shown when a location address is missing. Disabled because Text Management is turned off. Frontend text will use defaults.

Shown when location results are approximate. Disabled because Text Management is turned off. Frontend text will use defaults.

Success message after changing location via AJAX. Disabled because Text Management is turned off. Frontend text will use defaults.

Label used when the selector is set to show all products. Disabled because Text Management is turned off. Frontend text will use defaults.

Error shown when the selector security check fails. Disabled because Text Management is turned off. Frontend text will use defaults.

Error shown when the selected location is invalid. Disabled because Text Management is turned off. Frontend text will use defaults.

Error shown when a location slug does not exist. Disabled because Text Management is turned off. Frontend text will use defaults.

Alerts & Messages

General alert and status text used across the frontend.

Prompt shown when a location is required to add to cart. Disabled because Text Management is turned off. Frontend text will use defaults.

Badge text for out-of-stock products. Disabled because Text Management is turned off. Frontend text will use defaults.

Location Hours Restriction

Messages shown when purchases are restricted by business hours.

Shown when checkout is blocked during closed hours. Use %s for location name. Disabled because Text Management is turned off. Frontend text will use defaults.

Supplemental note that shows opening time. Use %s for time. Disabled because Text Management is turned off. Frontend text will use defaults.

Title used for restriction notices. Disabled because Text Management is turned off. Frontend text will use defaults.

Labels used in the variation-specific location info block displayed on single product and archive pages when Location Information is enabled in Settings → Inventory.

Product Visibility Rules

Configure advanced rules for product visibility based on locations.

Show Global Products

Show products that are not assigned to any specific location.

Product Priority Display

Set display priority for location-specific vs. global products.

Single Product Unavailable Behavior

Choose what happens when a customer visits a single product page URL but the product is not available in their selected location.

Out of Stock Product Display

Configure how products are displayed based on location availability and stock.

Show Out of Stock Products

How to display products that are out of stock at the selected location.

Stock Display Format

How to display stock information for location-specific products.

Admin Visibility Controls

Configure admin-specific visibility and management options.

Show All Products in Admin

Whether admins can see all products regardless of location restrictions.

Location Filtering Settings

Configure how strictly products are filtered by location throughout your store.

Strict Location Filtering

When enabled, users will only see products from their selected location. When disabled, all products will be visible.

Order Fulfillment

Configure how orders are processed and fulfilled from different locations.

Order Assignment Method

Choose how orders are assigned to fulfillment locations. In Manual Assignment mode, orders placed without a selected location are set to On Hold until a location is assigned. Manual Assignment is disabled while Location Wise Currency is enabled. If Manual was previously selected, Proximity Based will be used. Manual Assignment is disabled while Location Wise Currency is enabled. If Manual was previously selected, Proximity Based will be used.

Optional Location Selection (Manual/Inventory/Proximity)

Allow customers to optionally select a location when Manual, Inventory-Based, or Proximity-Based assignment is enabled. Location selection remains optional.

Order Notification Recipients

Who should receive order notifications.

Cross-Location Order Management

Configure how orders containing products from multiple locations are handled.

Allow Mixed-Location Cart

Allow customers to add products from different locations to their cart. When Enable Location Wise Currency is enabled, this option will be disabled.

Group Cart Items by Location

Group cart items by their store location for better visibility.

Cart Settings

Configure cart behavior for location-based shopping.

Allow Location Change in Cart

Allow customers to change the location for products in their cart if the product is available in multiple locations. When Enable Location Wise Currency is enabled, this option will be disabled.

Checkout Settings

Configure checkout address behavior for location-based shopping.

Split Order by Location

Create a separate order for each location when the cart contains items from multiple locations. Requires Allow Mixed-Location Cart to be enabled.

Unknown Location Items

Choose how to handle items without a valid location when orders are split by location. Enable Split Order by Location to configure this option.

Block checkout: Blocks checkout if any item lacks a valid location. Customers must update the cart or choose a valid location before placing the order.

Auto-Populate Customer Addresses

Automatically fill in customer addresses based on their detected location for a faster checkout process.

Inventory Management

Configure how inventory is managed across multiple locations.

Inventory Sync Mode

Choose how inventory is managed across multiple locations.

Low Stock Threshold

Trigger low-stock alert when location stock is less than or equal to this number (units).

Low Stock Notification Recipients

Who should receive low stock notifications.

Out of Stock Threshold

Trigger out-of-stock alert when location stock is less than or equal to this number (units).

General Settings

Configure general settings for location-based stock and price management.

Enable Location Stock

Enable or disable location-specific stock management.

Enable Location Pricing

Enable or disable location-specific pricing.

Enable Location Wise Currency

Enable location-specific currency and currency position based on selected location. Requires Enable Location Pricing. This option is automatically turned off when Enable Location Pricing is disabled. When enabled, mixed-location cart and location change in cart are disabled.

Enable Location Backorder

Enable or disable location-specific backorder management.

Enable Location Information

Enable or disable location-specific information management.

Enable Location by User Role







Select user roles for which location-specific information is enabled.

Shipping Payments Tax Discounts Reviews SEO

Location-Based Shipping

Configure shipping options based on product locations.

Enable Location-Based Shipping

Enable different shipping options based on product location.

Shipping Calculation Method

How shipping rates are calculated for multi-location orders.

Inter-Location Transfer Costs

Configure shipping costs for transferring products from one location to another. Leave empty or 0 for no transfer cost.

From → To Canada Chiang Mai Dubai France India Jhawtola Kandirpar New York test

Same Location

How Transfer Costs Work:

  • Each cell represents the cost to transfer products FROM one location TO another
  • Costs can be different in each direction (e.g., Location A → B may differ from B → A)
  • Leave empty or set to 0 for no transfer cost between specific locations
  • These costs are added to shipping when products need to be transferred between locations

Location-wise Payment Methods

Configure which payment methods are available for each store location.

Enable Location-wise Payment

Enable or disable payment method restrictions by location.

Location-based Tax Settings

Configure tax settings specific to each store location.

Enable Location-based Taxes

Apply different tax rates based on the product location.

WooCommerce taxes are currently disabled. To use location-based taxes, please enable taxes in WooCommerce settings first.

Tax Calculation for Mixed Cart

How taxes are calculated when cart contains products from multiple locations.

Location-based Discounts

Configure discount rules specific to each store location.

Enable Location Discounts

Allow different discount rules for each store location.

Location-Specific Coupon Codes

Allow coupon codes to be restricted to specific store locations.

Coupon Behavior for Cross Location

Control how coupons behave when carts contain products from multiple locations.

Location-based Product Reviews

Configure how product reviews are handled across different locations.

Location-Specific Reviews

Allow products to have different reviews based on location.

Show Location in Reviews

Display location information in product reviews.

Location SEO Settings

Configure SEO settings for location-based product pages.

Location in Meta Title

Include location name in product page meta titles.

Location in Meta Description

Include location information in product meta descriptions.

Location Structured Data

Add location information to product structured data for SEO.

Admin Notifications

Keep admins and browsers in sync with live status alerts, PWA notices, and floating summaries.

Real-time alerts

Enable background polling for live dashboard metrics and alerts.

Floating notifications

Show brief floating toasts in the admin header when new alerts arrive.

Floating position

Choose where floating alerts should appear.

Size

Choose the size of floating notifications.

Floating duration (milliseconds)

How long floating alerts remain visible.

Notification template

Use {event} and {message} placeholders to customize floating and PWA text.

Browser push (PWA)

Send desktop/mobile notifications through a registered service worker.

Test PWA Notification
Test Notifications
Quick tests Use this to verify floating notifications and email delivery/routing.
This sends a simple test email and reports the resolved recipient(s).
Poll interval (milliseconds)

How often to check for new alerts (minimum 5000ms). Lower values increase server load.

Notification style

Choose the visual style for floating notifications.

Enable notification sound

Play a sound when new notifications arrive.

Location-based Email Notifications

Configure email notifications based on store locations.

Location-Specific Email Templates

Use different email templates for different store locations.

Include Location Logo in Emails

Include the store location logo in order emails.

Location-Specific Email Recipients

Send order notifications to location-specific email addresses.

Email Notification — Important Notes

When enabled, order and pickup notifications will be sent to the location manager (if assigned) and the location contact email. Admin recipients depend on your notification settings.

Available placeholders for email templates:

{order_store_location} {store_location_address} {store_location_phone} {store_location_email} {store_location_logo} {store_manager_name}

Example usage in an email template:

    Order received for: {order_store_location}
Address: {store_location_address}
Contact: {store_location_phone} / {store_location_email}
Manager: {store_manager_name}

{store_location_logo}

If a location logo is not available the site/store default will be used. For full integration details and more placeholders, see the plugin documentation.

Social Notifications

Manage social/webhook alerts per platform. Add multiple destinations and test instantly.

Social Notifications

Enable alerts and choose which events to send to your social/webhook destinations.

Social Channels

Add one or more destinations. Use webhook URL for Slack/Teams/Discord/Custom; use chat ID/token for Telegram.

No social media added yet. Click “Add Social Media” to create one.
How to connect Microsoft Teams
  1. In Teams, open the channel → More options (...) → Connectors/Apps → search “Incoming Webhook”.
  2. Click Add/Create, name it (e.g., “Store Alerts”), optionally upload an icon, then click Create.
  3. Copy the webhook URL, paste it into the Webhook URL field above, and save settings.
  4. Enable the events you want (orders, stock alerts, digest, site monitor), then click Test.

Watch: Teams setup  |  Microsoft Docs: Incoming Webhook

How to connect Slack
  1. In Slack, go to Settings & administration → Manage apps → search “Incoming WebHooks” and click Add.
  2. Choose the channel for alerts and click Add Incoming Webhook Integration (or Add Configuration).
  3. Copy the webhook URL, paste it into the Webhook URL field above, and save.
  4. Enable desired events and click Test.

Slack Docs: Incoming Webhooks

How to connect Discord
  1. In Discord, Server Settings → Integrations → Webhooks → New Webhook.
  2. Select the target channel and copy the webhook URL.
  3. Paste the URL into the Webhook field above and save settings.
  4. Enable events and click Test.

Discord Docs: Webhooks

How to connect Telegram
  1. Create a bot via @BotFather and copy the bot token.
  2. Start a chat with @userinfobot (or add the bot to a group) to get chat ID.
  3. Enter chat ID and bot token above (or use the global token), then save.
  4. Enable events and click Test.

Telegram Docs: Bots API

How to connect a Custom Webhook
  1. Use any HTTPS endpoint that accepts a JSON POST payload with a “text” field.
  2. Paste the endpoint URL above and save.
  3. Enable events and click Test.

Example: Custom Webhook setup guide

User Experience Settings

Configure how customers interact with location-based features.

Require Location Selection

Require customers to select a location before adding products to the cart.

Location Switching Behavior

How to handle cart contents when a customer changes their location.

Location Change Notification

Display a notification when a customer changes their location.

Location-based Customer Insights

Configure customer analytics and insights based on location data.

Enable Customer Location Tracking

Track and analyze customer preferences by location.

Customer Location History

How to store customer location selection history.

Location-based Recommendations

Show product recommendations based on location popularity.

Location Selection Display

Configure how the location selector appears to customers.

Display Location on Single Product

Show current location on single product pages.

Location Display Position

Where to display the current location on single product pages.

Location Selector Layout
Hide Out of Stock Locations

Hide locations that are out of stock for the current product in the selector.

Product Filter

Control the location-based product filter display on shop and archive pages.

Enable Product Filter

Enable or disable automatic product filter display on shop/archive pages. Use shortcode [mulopimfwc_product_filter] to place it manually. Available only when Manual, Inventory-Based, or Proximity-Based assignment is enabled and Optional Location Selection is disabled.

RecommendedDynamic AJAX Product Filters for WooCommerce

For the best filtering experience, use our Dynamic AJAX Product Filters for WooCommerce. It is fast, works with all themes and builders, and delivers smooth filtering without full page reloads.

FastTheme FriendlyBuilder Ready
Learn More

Default Location/Warehouse

Set a default location/warehouse that will be used when popup is not enabled and no location is selected.

Default Location/Warehouse

Select a default location/warehouse. This location will be used automatically when popup is disabled and no location cookie is set.

Popup Priority Active

Popup is currently enabled and a default location/warehouse is selected. Customers will follow popup-based location selection first. This default location is applied when popup is turned off.

Store Locator

Configure store locator functionality and integration.

Enable Store Locator

Enable store locator with map functionality.

Map Provider

Select which map provider to use for the store locator.

Default Map Zoom Level

Default zoom level for the store locator map (1-19).

Map Animation Type

Choose how the map animates when switching between locations. Fly To provides smooth camera movement, Set View offers animated transitions, and Instant provides immediate updates.

Map Animation Duration (seconds)

Duration of the map animation in seconds (0-5). Only applies when animation is enabled.

Store Locator Display Position

Configure where to display the store locator on different pages.

Single Product Page Position

Choose where to display the store locator on single product pages.

Location Archive Page Position

Choose where to display the store locator on location archive pages.

Location Hours & Availability

Configure business hours and availability for each store location.

Enable Business Hours

Enable management of business hours for each location.

Display Hours on Location Archive

Show store hours on Location Archive pages next to location information.

Restrict Purchasing to Open Hours

Only allow purchases when the store location is open.

Location URL Settings

Configure how location information appears in URLs.

Enable Location in URLs

Include location information in product and category URLs.

URL Location Format

How to format location information in URLs.

Location URL Prefix

URL Prefix (e.g., "store" for store-name.example.com):

After changes, visit Settings > Permalinks and click "Save Changes" to flush rewrite rules.

Advanced Location Pickup Settings

Configure advanced settings for in-store pickup functionality.

Enable Location Pickup

Enable in-store pickup option for products at specific locations.

Pickup Preparation Time (Hours)

Default preparation time in hours before an order is ready for pickup.

Advanced Settings

Advanced configuration options for location management.

Location Cookie Expiry (Days)

Number of days to remember user's location choice (1-365).

Contribute to Plugincy

We collect non-sensitive technical details from your website, like the PHP version and features usage, to help us troubleshoot issues faster, make informed development decisions, and build features that truly benefit you.

Cache Cleanup

Clear cached dashboard metrics, investment/revenue charts, and filter data. Useful after price or stock updates.

Import & Export Settings

Configure options for importing and exporting location-based product data.

Export Plugin Settings

Export all plugin settings as a JSON file for backup or migration.

Import Plugin Settings

Import plugin settings from a previously exported JSON file.

Export Products with Location Data

Export/import versioned canonical CSV for full WooCommerce product data plus location-wise data. Import runs dry-run first, then asks for confirmation.

API & Webhooks

Configure REST API endpoints and webhooks for syncing inventory data from external systems like WMS or POS.

API & Webhooks Configuration

API Key

Generate an API key to authenticate REST API requests.
Webhook Secret

Generate a webhook secret to authenticate webhook requests from external systems.
Log Webhooks

Webhook logs are saved to wp-content/uploads/mulopimfwc-webhook-log-YYYY-MM-DD.log

Active

Reset Settings

This will reset all plugin settings to their default values. This action cannot be undone. Please make sure you have exported your settings if you want to restore them later.

Location Selector Shortcode

Use this shortcode to display the location selector on any page or post. Copy and paste it into the WordPress editor or use it in your template files.

Shortcode
[mulopimfwc_store_location_selector enable_user_locations="on"]

Available Parameters

title
Set custom selector title
! [mulopimfwc_store_location_selector title="Select Your Location"]
show_title
on / off - Display title
! [mulopimfwc_store_location_selector show_title="on"]
use_select2
on / off - Enhanced dropdown
! [mulopimfwc_store_location_selector use_select2="on"]
herichical
on / off / seperately
! [mulopimfwc_store_location_selector herichical="on"]
show_count
on / off - Show item count
! [mulopimfwc_store_location_selector show_count="on"]
class
Custom CSS class name
! [mulopimfwc_store_location_selector class="my-custom-class"]
enable_user_locations
on / off - User locations
! [mulopimfwc_store_location_selector enable_user_locations="off"]
max_width
Maximum width in pixels
! [mulopimfwc_store_location_selector max_width="300"]
multi_line
on / off - Multi-line display
! [mulopimfwc_store_location_selector multi_line="on"]

Pro Tip: You can modify the parameter values directly in the shortcode to customize the location selector appearance and behavior.

Advanced

API & Webhook Integration: Bulk Inventory Sync

Complete guide to integrating your WMS, POS, or ERP systems with the plugin using REST API endpoints and webhooks. Learn how to sync inventory in bulk via CSV or API, set up real-time webhook updates, generate API keys, configure authentication, and automate inventory management across multiple locations. This tutorial covers bulk operations, single product updates, CSV import/export, webhook configuration, and best practices for external system integration.

Topics Covered
REST API Webhooks WMS Integration POS Integration Bulk Sync CSV Import Inventory Automation API Authentication
Usage Examples
How to Get API Key & Webhook Secret GET
Endpoint: WordPress Admin → Multi Location Products → API & Webhooks
Steps / Request Body:
Step 1:Navigate to WordPress Admin Dashboard
Step 2:Go to: Multi Location Products → API & Webhooks
Step 3:Scroll to "API Authentication" section
Step 4:Click "Generate API Key" button (if no key exists)
Step 5:Copy the generated API key immediately
Step 6:Click "Generate Webhook Secret" button (if no secret exists)
Step 7:Copy the generated webhook secret immediately
Step 8:Store both keys securely (they are shown only once)
Important note:If you lose your keys, you must generate new ones. Old keys will no longer work.
Response: 
{
    "api_key_location": "Displayed in \"API Key\" field after generation",
    "webhook_secret_location": "Displayed in \"Webhook Secret\" field after generation",
    "security_warning": "Keys are displayed only once for security. Copy them immediately.",
    "usage_api_key": "Use in X-API-Key header for REST API requests",
    "usage_webhook_secret": "Use in X-Webhook-Secret header for webhook requests"
}
Bulk Sync Example POST
Endpoint: /mulopimfwc/v1/inventory/bulk-sync
Headers: 
Content-Type: application/json
X-API-Key: your-api-key-here
Steps / Request Body: 
{
    "items": [
        {
            "sku": "PROD-001",
            "location_slug": "main-store",
            "stock": 100,
            "regular_price": "29.99",
            "sale_price": "24.99"
        },
        {
            "product_id": 123,
            "location_id": 5,
            "stock": 50,
            "regular_price": "19.99"
        }
    ]
}
Response: 
{
    "success": true,
    "message": "Processed 2 items. 2 succeeded, 0 failed.",
    "results": {
        "success": 2,
        "failed": 0,
        "errors": []
    }
}
Single Product Update Example POST
Endpoint: /mulopimfwc/v1/inventory/update
Headers: 
Content-Type: application/json
X-API-Key: your-api-key-here
Steps / Request Body: 
{
    "product_id": 456,
    "location_id": 3,
    "stock": 25,
    "regular_price": "39.99",
    "sale_price": "34.99",
    "backorders": "no",
    "disabled": false
}
Response: 
{
    "success": true,
    "message": "Inventory updated successfully.",
    "data": {
        "product_id": 456,
        "location_id": 3,
        "sku": "PROD-456"
    }
}
Export Inventory CSV Example GET
Endpoint: /mulopimfwc/v1/inventory/export?format=csv&location_id=5
Headers: 
X-API-Key: your-api-key-here
Response: 
{
    "note": "Returns CSV file download with headers: product_id, sku, product_name, location_id, location_slug, location_name, stock, regular_price, sale_price, backorders, disabled"
}
Export Inventory JSON Example GET
Endpoint: /mulopimfwc/v1/inventory/export?format=json
Headers: 
X-API-Key: your-api-key-here
Response: 
{
    "success": true,
    "count": 150,
    "data": [
        {
            "product_id": 123,
            "sku": "PROD-001",
            "product_name": "Sample Product",
            "location_id": 5,
            "location_slug": "main-store",
            "location_name": "Main Store",
            "stock": 100,
            "regular_price": "29.99",
            "sale_price": "24.99",
            "backorders": "no",
            "disabled": false
        }
    ]
}
Webhook Example POST
Endpoint: /mulopimfwc/v1/webhook/inventory-update
Headers: 
Content-Type: application/json
X-Webhook-Secret: your-webhook-secret-here
Steps / Request Body: 
{
    "sku": "PROD-001",
    "location_slug": "main-store",
    "stock": 75
}
Response: 
{
    "success": true,
    "message": "Inventory updated via webhook.",
    "data": {
        "product_id": 123,
        "location_id": 5,
        "sku": "PROD-001"
    }
}
Get Locations Example GET
Endpoint: /mulopimfwc/v1/locations
Headers: 
X-API-Key: your-api-key-here
Response: 
{
    "success": true,
    "count": 3,
    "data": [
        {
            "id": 5,
            "slug": "main-store",
            "name": "Main Store",
            "description": "Our primary retail location"
        },
        {
            "id": 6,
            "slug": "warehouse",
            "name": "Warehouse",
            "description": "Distribution center"
        }
    ]
}
Get Products Example GET
Endpoint: /mulopimfwc/v1/products
Headers: 
X-API-Key: your-api-key-here
15
3:20
